mahadbt-baksy:
*Id*:
65

*Scheme Code*:
mahadbt-baksy

*Sponsor*:
State

*Scheme Provider*:
Maha-DBT

*Scheme Name*:
Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Krushi Swavalamban Yojana (BAKSY)

*Main Component*:
Providing Irrigation Fscilities to the eligible beneficiaries of Scheduled Cast (SC) category

*Sub Component*:
1. New Irrigation Well
2. Old Well Repair
3. Plastic Lining of Farm Ponds
4. Inwell Boring
5. Electricity Connection
6. Pump Set (Diesel/Electric)
7. Solar pump (instead of electrical connection Charges and pump set)
8. HDPE/PVC Pipe (New Item)
9. Sprinkler Irrigation Set (Supplementary Grant) AND
     Drip Irrigation Set Supplementary Grant
10. Farm Machinery (bullock-drawn/tractor-drawn implements)
11. Backyard / Kitchen Garden

*Introduction*:
The Scheduled Caste Sub-Plan (Special Component Scheme(scp)) was being implemented from the year 1982-83 to increase the income of Scheduled Caste / New-Buddhist farmers and improve their standard of living. Since the said scheme has been implemented for a long period of time, the GOVERNMENT OF MAHARASHTRA has decided to implement the Special Component Scheme for Scheduled Caste / Neo-Buddhist farmers since 1982-83 and the recommendations of the Agriculture Commissionerate dated 27/04/2016 on the report dated 19/04/2016 and special component scheme the announced by the Hon'ble Minister (Finance). Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana is not being implemented separately, but the current special component scheme, Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana, is being implemented from 2016-17 as per the changed criteria, taking into account the needs of the changed situation and maintaining soil moisture.
Also, the government has approved the amendment in the financial criteria and inclusion of new components under the said scheme as per the government decision dated 01 October, 2024.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    beneficiary Selection criteria are as follows
1.  Scheme is only for Scheduled Caste/ Nav Baudha farmers.
2.  Scheme is implemented in all districts of Maharashtra (Except Mumbai districts).
3. At field level  scheme is under Implementation by department of Agriculture, Zilha Parishad.
4. Funds are released from Social Justice Department to concerned  District Collector and then made available to concern Zilha Parishad.
5. Application ,Selection of beneficiaries upto payment of subsidy all process are done through MAHADBT Portal through online system.                                                                  "

*Benefits*:
Benefit provided on subsidy basis as per the norms of components, through online system. The said scheme shall be implemented in the financial year 2025-26 as follows. In the said scheme, grants shall be admissible for the following components in the year 2025-26 up to the maximum limit of the amount mentioned below.
1. For New Irrigation- Well Maximum subsidy ammount limit is Rs.4,00,000/ - is admissible.
2. For Old Well Repair - Maximum subsidy ammount limit is 1,00,000/- is admissible
3. Plastic Lining of Farm Ponds-Grant shall be admissible up to 90% of the amount calculated as per the prevailing financial parameters of the National Horticulture Mission Guideline and the size of the farm pond or actual cost or Rs. 2,00,000/- whichever is less is admissible.
4.Inwell Boring- Maximum subsidy ammount limit is 40,000/- is admissible
5. Electricity Connection-Maximum subsidy ammount limit is Amount 20,000/- or actual paid amount whichever is less is admissible.
6. Pump Set (Diesel/Electric)(Diesel Engine - New Item) For pump sets up to 10 HP capacity, grant shall be admissible up to 90% of the prevailing financial parameters or actual cost or Rs. 40,000/- whichever is less is admissible.
7. Grant Solar pump (instead of electrical connection and pump set)- Maximum subsidy will be admissible up to 90% of the prevailing financial criteria or actual expenditure incurred or Rs. 50,000/- whichever is less is admissible.
8. HDPE/PVC Pipe (New Item)- Under the Food and Nutrition Security Scheme - Food Crops Scheme, subsidy of 100% of the prevailing economic standard or actual cost incurred per meter or Rs. 50,000/- whichever is less will be admissible.
9.1 For Micro irrigation system Under Sprinkler Irrigation Set (Supplementary Grant) 9.1.1) Under the Per Drop More Crop Scheme, (1) 55% for small/very small landholders + 25% from the Chief Minister's Sustainable Irrigation Scheme + 10% from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana and9.1.2) 45% for large landholders + 30% from the Chief Minister's Sustainable Irrigation Scheme + 15% from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana or Rs. 47,000/- whichever is less will be admissible as subsidy. (Total subsidy limit of 90 percent) is admissible
9.2 For Drip Irrigation Set Supplementary Grant 9.2.1 ) Under the Per Drop More Crop Scheme, (1) 55% for small/very small landholders + 25% from the Chief Minister's Sustainable Irrigation Scheme + 10% from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana and 9.2.2) 45% for large landholders + 30% from the Chief Minister's Sustainable Irrigation Scheme + 15% from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Krishi Swavalamban Yojana or Rs. 97,000/- whichever is less will be admissible as subsidy.(Total subsidy limit of 90 percent) is admissible
10. Farm Machinery (bullock-drawn/tractor-drawn implements) (New item)maximum subsidy limit is 50,000/- is admissible
11. Backyard / Kitchen Garden (New Item) maximum subsidy limit is 50,000/- is admissible .

*Eligibility*:
1. Scheme is only for Scheduled Tribe farmers. Caste  certificate is essential.
2. Land holding limit is 0.20 ha. to  6.00 ha.(for benefit of New well component minimum 0.40 ha. land essential)
3.1 It will be mandatory for the beneficiary to be a farmer belonging to the Scheduled Caste / Neo-Buddhist category.
3.2 The farmer must have a caste certificate issued by the competent authority.
3.3 Farmers residing the Outside the Nagar Panchayat, Nagarpalika and Mahanagarpalika is necessary to have 7/12 certificate and 8A extract of land tenure in the name of the farmer. ( Outside the Nagar Panchayat, Nagarpalika and Mahanagarpalika area (Reference Government Letter No. Sankirna- 2017/Pr.No.142/3A, dated 24 April 2018))
3.4 The beneficiaries must have their own Aadhaar link active bank account .
3.5 The beneficiaries below the poverty line will be given  first priority.
3.6 The beneficiary farmer must have a minimum of 0.40 hectares and a maximum of 6 hectares of agricultural land. If the beneficiary's land is in a remote and fragmented area and two or more beneficiaries with less than 0.40 hectares of land come together and their combined land is at least 0.40 hectares, then if they sign an agreement, they will be eligible for the benefit of the scheme. Similarly, the condition of maximum 6.0 hectares of land will not be applicable to the beneficiaries below the poverty line.
3.7 Once the full benefit of the relevant scheme is taken, the benefit of this scheme will not be payable to the same beneficiary or family for the next five years. The beneficiary who has taken the benefit of the new well component will be eligible for the benefit of the old well repair component after 20 years.
If the benefit has been given under the Special Component Scheme for similar agricultural development schemes and the scheme implemented from the funds made available under the SCA of the Central Government and Article 275 (A) of the Constitution, the benefit provided under this scheme will not be admissible.

*Required Documents*:
1. Caste Certificate of SC category issued by compitant authority
2. current 7/12 report having Land holding limit is 0.20 ha. to 6.00 ha.(for benefit of New well component minimum 0.40 ha. land essential) within the period of
3 months 3.GSDA Certificates
4. There is no income limit for this scheme

*Application Process*:
Application is to done online on Mahadbt portal as follows
1. Applications from farmers are being invited for the said scheme on the Maha-DBT portal website mahadbt.maharashtra.gov.in/farmer/login /login and the applications received within the limit of lakhs received by the concerned talukas / blocks will be selected online on a first come, first served basis (fcfs). After selection, all the proceedings up to the payment of subsidy to the beneficiaries should be done online. In this regard, proceedings from acceptance of application to payment of subsidy to the beneficiaries should be done as mentioned in the referenced government decision dated 04/11/2020.
2. The Agricultural Officer (Agriculture), P.S., should conduct a site inspection of the area of the beneficiary selected for the new well and old well repair on a first come, first served basis and check the technical feasibility. After the inspection, if the location of the beneficiary's new well and the proposal for repair of the old well of the beneficiary is technically suitable, the Agricultural Development Officer should approve the budget for this matter and give prior approval to the proposals of the concerned beneficiary within a maximum of 30 days after uploading the documents. Similarly, the selection of the beneficiaries whose proposals are found to be technically unsuitable during the site inspection should be cancelled.
3. Beneficiaries selected for other matters other than new well and old well repair under the scheme should be given prior approval within a maximum of 30 days after uploading the documents and they should be given the benefit of the scheme in the current financial year 2025-26 itself.
4. The prior approval of the beneficiaries who do not implement the matters under the scheme within the prescribed period should be cancelled.
5. While selecting individual beneficiary, priority will be given to women and disabled beneficiaries. Out of the total sanctioned funds, 5 percent of the funds should be given priority to be spent on disabled beneficiaries and 30 percent of the funds should be spent on women beneficiaries.
6. As per the demand of the final selected beneficiaries as per the target of the taluka & district, a draft plan of the district should be prepared within 7 days as per Appendix No. 1, component-wise and beneficiary-wise. The responsibility of preparing the draft plan will be the responsibility of the Agricultural Development Officer.

Death or loss of organ / organs due to accident.

*Introduction*:
1. The scheme is funded by Government of Maharashtra                                                             2. Farmers are prone to various accidents while performing farming business. Due to death/ disability of earning head of family, the family members of farmers have to face critical financial situation. In order to give financial support to the aggrieved family members of the ill-fated farmers the State Govt. has provided accident insurance cover through this scheme.
3. Compensation up to Rs.2 lakhs is provided.
4. Farmer and one of his family members is covered under the scheme.

*Benefits*:
In case of accidental death or permanent disability of farmer or one family member of farmer within the age  of 10 to 75 years, Finalcial assistance upto Rs. 2 lakh per case is given to farmers family. Taluka level committee under the chairmanship of Tahsildar sanction the proposal and benefit given to the farmers family through DBT.

*Eligibility*:
All farmers in the age group of 10 to 75 . and one family member of the farmers family are eligible for assistance under this scheme

*Required Documents*:
A person registered as farmer as evidenced by 7/12 extract between the age of 10 -75
 years is eligible.

*Application Process*:
Submit the complete proposal and documents to Taluka Agriculture Officer within 30 days of the incident.

National Food Security and Nutrition Mission (NFSNM) Commercial Crop- Cotton Development Scheme

*Main Component*:
1) Crop Demonstration 2) Cotton Seed production 3)  Integrated Pest Management  4)  On Line Pest Monitoring and Advisory Services  5) State level training  6) Local Initiatives - a) Early termination & stubble removal demonstration b) Biochar

*Sub Component*:
1) Crop Demonstration -a) Front- Line Demonstation (FLD) on Intercropping (Mung, Udid) , b) Trials on High Density Planting Systems , c) Front- Line Demonstation (FLD) on Integrated Crop Management (ICM)
2) Cotton Seed production - FLD on Desi & Extra Long Staple -(ELS) cotton/ ELS cotton Seed Production
3)  Integrated Pest Management (IPM) -Distribution of plant  protection chemicals and Bio agents
4) On Line Pest Monitoring and Advisory Services (OPMAS)
5) State level training
6) Local Initiatives - Early termination & stubble removal demonstration

*Introduction*:
The National Food Security Mission (NFSNM) has been implementing the cotton development programme under the commercial cotton programme since 2014-15. The scheme will be implemented under the name of National Food Security and Nutrition Mission (NFSNM) from 2025-26. It includes measures such as early-maturing (production of extra-long staple cotton seed), early-maturing (intercropping of cotton, urad etc.), intensive larval management of straight varieties of cotton, distribution of crop protection drugs and bioagents, Online Pest Monitoring and Advisory Services (OPMAS) and state-level monitoring, field-based pest control, early-maturing and biochar. For this, the program will be implemented in the ratio of Center: State 60:40 percent. Along with foodgrains, cotton cultivation has been included in the scheme to increase the cultivation of non-food crops.

*Benefits*:
1) Front- Line Demonstation (FLD) on Intercropping (Mung, Udid)- Rs. 8000/ha
2) Trials on High Density Planting Systems Rs. - 10000/ha
3) Front- Line Demonstation (FLD) on Integrated Crop Management (ICM) -Rs.-Rs.8000/ha.
4) Cotton Seed production - FLD on Desi & Extra Long Staple -(ELS) cotton/ ELS cotton Seed Production -Rs.9000/ha.
5)Distribution of plant  protection chemicals and Bio agents -Rs. 500/ha or 50% of the cost (whichever is less)
6) On Line Pest Monitoring and Advisory Services (OPMAS) -Rs. 8000/ha.
7) State level trainings (20 participants X 2 days) - Rs. 40000/traning.
8) Early termination & stubble removal demonstration- Rs. 5000/ha.
7) Biochar - Rs. 10000/Unit.

*Eligibility*:
1) Crop Demonstration- Group Beneficiary
2) Biachar - Indiviual
3) Traning - Agri Depaetment Staff.

*Required Documents*:
1) It is mandatory for the farmers to be given subsidy to these politicians to be registered on Agristack.
2) Farmer ID is mandatory .

2) Biochar -MAHADBT Portal

3) Tissue culture plantlets

 6)Local Initiatives-

b) Supercane Nursery

*Introduction*:
From the year 2014-15, a sugarcane cultivation program is being implemented under the National Food Security Mission - Commercial Crops. The program will be implemented as follows: Center: State, 60: 40 percent.
Sugarcane is an important cash crop in Maharashtra. The state has favorable climate conditions for sugarcane growth. Maharashtra is leading in the country in terms of sugar production and sugar yield. But the average per hectare sugarcane productivity is low. Therefore, it is important to make sugarcane production technology accessible to maximum farmers. By reducing production costs by adding modern technology to the field, farmers will be able to get sustainable income from sugarcane cultivation. With a view to reducing the cost of sugarcane production and increasing productivity, a sugarcane expansion program is being implemented in the state under the National Food Security and Nutrition Mission (NFSNM) in the year 2025-26.

*Benefits*:
1) Demonstration intercropping and single bud chip technology with Sugarcane -Rs. 9000/ha.
2) Assistance for breeder seed production -Rs. 40000/ha.
3)  Tissue culture plantlets - Rs. 3.5/ Seedling
4) State level training (20 participants X 2 days) - Rs.40000/Traning)
5) Distribution of plant protection Chemicals and Bioagents -Rs. 500/ha or 50% of the cost (whichever is less)
6) Local Initiatives-
 a) Ratoon and Thrash Management demonstration - Rs. 6000/ha.
 b)Supercane Nursery-Rs. 10000/Unit

*Eligibility*:
1) Crop Demonstration- Group Beneficiary
2) Biachar - Indiviual Farmer

*Introduction*:
Under this project, priority has been given to the bamboo plantation component under the agroforestry system. Bamboo plantation under the project will help in stabilizing carbon gases that cause climate change, and will also contribute to raising the economic standard of living of farmers by establishing other small bamboo-based industries.

*Benefits*:
1. To increase tree cover, conserve soil organic matter, and increase productivity from crops and cropping systems to reduce the adverse effects of carbon emissions in the atmosphere.

*Main Component*:

*Sub Component*:
Farm Pond Lining

*Introduction*:
Most of the dryland farming in the state is dependent on rainfall, and regions like Marathwada, Vidarbha, and East Vidarbha frequently experience low rainfall, uneven distribution, and long dry spells. Due to the lack of irrigation, there is a significant reduction in crop production, and sometimes crops are completely lost, leading to financial losses for farmers. On the other hand, when it rains heavily, runoff water flows through streams and nullahs and goes to waste. As a sustainable solution to this problem, it is very important to store the runoff rainwater in farm ponds and use it for protective irrigation. However, to retain the stored water for a longer period and to prevent wastage of water, lining of the farm ponds is necessary. Farm pond lining prevents water seepage, helps in retaining water, and allows farmers to use it for agriculture as needed. This increases productivity, averts financial losses, and makes farming more sustainable. Therefore, the farm pond lining component is being implemented under this project.

*Benefits*:
1. To empower small and marginal farmers in the villages selected under the project to adapt to the situation arising from climate change. 
2. To create a facility for protective irrigation, increase the area under a second crop, and increase the farmer's income. 
3. To encourage supplementary farming activities like horticulture, vegetable crops, pisciculture, orchards, and animal husbandry through farm ponds.

*Eligibility*:
1. Farmers who have received benefits from any other scheme for this component will not receive benefits under this scheme again.
2. If the farmer has constructed a pond without inlets and outlets according to size from any other government scheme, they will be eligible for the lining component.
3. The benefits of this component under the project will be available to beneficiaries who own up to 5 hectares of land.
4. Compensation for damage will not be allowed if any harm occurs to the pond due to natural disasters.

*Main Component*:
Organic Production Unit

*Sub Component*:
1. Vermicompost Production Unit 
2. NADEP Compost Production Unit

*Introduction*:
Under the organic farming system, conserving nature, reducing crop production costs, and increasing the use of alternative organic and biological inputs for hazardous chemicals like chemical fertilizers, pesticides, herbicides, and hormones is the need of the hour. Vermicompost is made from farm waste, plant-based materials, and cow dung by earthworms. Vermicompost contains various bacteria, enzymes, vitamins, and other useful chemicals, which have a positive effect on plant growth. 
Similarly, processing farm waste through composting results in the decomposition of its organic matter through biological methods by microorganisms and earthworms, producing excellent humus-like organic compost. If this compost is used extensively in the fields, it will significantly improve soil health and greatly boost agricultural production. It is the need of the hour to prepare organic manure from all plant-based materials generated in the field and return it to the field. Organic manure improves soil texture and water retention capacity, ensuring an adequate supply of nutrients. The soil remains friable, which keeps it aerated. It helps in the growth of beneficial microorganisms in the farm. 
In this regard, considering the above matter, it is proposed to implement the Vermicompost Production Unit and NADEP Compost Production Unit as individual benefit components.

*Benefits*:
1.To enable small and marginal farmers in the village clusters included in the Nanaji Deshmukh Krishi Sanjivani Prakalp to adapt to the situation arising from climate change.
2. To produce nutritious food grains.
3. To practice agriculture by making proper use of natural elements without harming them.
4. To increase soil fertility and maintain it for a long time.
5. To reuse local resources in agriculture.
6. To reduce the cost of agricultural production and increase the net income of the farmer.

1. Farmers who have received benefits from any other scheme for this component will not receive benefits again under this scheme.

The use of quality seeds is essential for increasing the production and productivity of major crops grown in the project area. For this, it is imperative to increase the seed replacement rate for pulses, oilseeds, and cereal crops in the project area. Therefore, special efforts are being made under the project to make seeds of climate-resilient varieties of major crops available to farmers. In climate-resilient varieties, emphasis should be placed on short-duration varieties, pest and disease-resistant or tolerant varieties, drought-tolerant varieties, etc. Additionally, the quality of the produced variety must also be appropriate for the market.

Along with this, it is essential to use the available groundwater sparingly and in a scientific manner. *Id*:
73

*Scheme Code*:
state-agri-award

*Sponsor*:
State

*Scheme Provider*:
DSAO

*Scheme Name*:
Various Agriculture Awards (Vividh Krushi Puraskar)

*Main Component*:
Various Agriculture Awards (Vividh Krushi Puraskar)

*Introduction*:
Through the Department of Agriculture, Government of Maharashtra, the farmers who have done remarkable work in the agriculture and allied sectors as well as the horticulture sector in the state and who have contributed to the increase in agricultural production and income, as well as the individuals/institutions/groups who have done valuable work in agricultural extension, are honored with the Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Ratna Award, Vasantrao Naik Krishi Bhushan Award, Jijamata Krishi Bhushan Award, Krishi Bhushan (Organic Agriculture) Award, Udyan Pandit Award, Vasantrao Naik Sheti Mitra Award, Vasantrao Naik Shetinishtha Award, Yuva Shetkari Award and the officers/employees who have done remarkable work in the agriculture departments are honored with the Padmashri Dr. Vitthalrao Vikhe Patil Krishi Sewaratna Award.

*Benefits*:
Amount in Rs., Souvenir (trophy), certificate of appreciation and felicitation with spouse/husband. Amount per Award is as follow-
 1 Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Ratna Award-Rs. 300000/-
 2 Vasantrao Naik Krishi Bhushan Award-Rs. 200000/-
 3 Jijamata Krishi Bhushan Award-Rs. 200000/-
 4 Organic Agriculture Krishi Bhushan Award-Rs. 200000/-
 5 Udyan Pandit Award-Rs. 100000/-
 6 Yuva Shetkari Award-Rs. 120000/-
 7 Vasantrao Naik Shetkari Mittra Award-Rs. 120000/-
 8 Vasantrao Naik Shetkari Nishitsha Shetkari Award (General Group)-Rs. 44000/-
 9 Vasantrao Naik Shetkari Nishitsha Shetkari Award (Tribal Group)-Rs. 44000/-
 10 Padmashree Dr.Vitthalrao Vikhe Patil Krishi Sewaratna Award-Rs. 0/-

*Eligibility*:
1) The award should be given to a person/group/organization doing organizational work in the agricultural sector. The work done by the proposed person/group/organization should be indicative and highly noteworthy for the entire state.
 2) In case of Dr. Punjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Ratna Award and Vasantrao Naik Krishi Bhushan Award, the proposed person/group/organization should have received any agricultural award from the Central or State Government.
 3) The farmer should be owner of his farm and farming should be his main occupation along with his family.
 4) He should have done remarkable work and earned more profit by participating in successful production, nursery, seed production, organic input production and use, post-harvest management, grading, packhouse, storage, value addition, processing, marketing, export etc.
 5) The farmer to be selected for the award should be one who guides the farmers in the area regarding mechanization, drip irrigation, cow dung gas, dairy farming, organic farming, vermicompost unit, agricultural processing, modern farming methods, post-harvest management, marketing, export etc. Along with this, he should be one who participates in crop competitions, exhibitions, fairs, visits to research institutes etc. and participates in the promotion and dissemination of modern agricultural technology and should also encourage the farmers in the area to participate in such activities. Details of participation/award etc. in crop competitions, exhibitions etc. should be given along with the proposal along with evidence.
 6) The gap between two state-level awards given by the Agriculture Department of the Government should be at least 05 years in case of Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Ratna Award and in case of other awards, the gap should be at least 03 years.
 7) A self-declared affidavit should be obtained from the concerned that he/she is not working in a Central/State Government/Semi-Government or Government-approved organization or is not a retired officer/employee, and is not receiving service pension or any kind of regular honorarium.
 8) If the farmer/organization has received the award presented earlier, he/she will not be eligible for the same award again.
 9) The concerned must submit the original copy of the character certificate of the District Superintendent of Police Office, self-declaration (Form - D - enclosed), 7/12, 8-A along with the proposal.
 10) All women farmers in the state will be eligible for Jijamata Krishi Bhushan Award but the wife will not be able to apply again for the award received by her husband.
 11) For the Krushi Bhushan (Organic Farming) Award, organic farming should be certified by PGS or other recognized organization. Should have received training in organic farming. Should have adopted organic farming methods for at least five years.
 12) For the Vasantrao Naik Shetimitra Award, individuals/organizations /newspapers/weeklies/magazines/television channels etc. who have contributed to agricultural development by providing research articles in agricultural daily/weekly/monthly newspapers, publicity of agricultural schemes, success stories of farmers etc. and by using social media like print/electronic and guiding maximum farmers should be considered for this award.
 13) The farmer to be selected for the Udyanpandit Award should be one who grows horticultural crops (fruits, vegetables, flowers, spice crops, aromatic medicinal plants, etc.) in a modern way, as this is the main condition, the proposed farmer should have a farm in his name. For this, it is also necessary to have records of horticultural crops in the current year as per the proposal details on 7/12 and 8-A along with the proposal. He should have made a notable contribution in the field of horticulture.
 14) The age of the farmer should be between 18 and 40 years at the time of submitting the proposal for the Yuva Shetkari Award.

*Required Documents*:
1) Original copy of the character certificate of the District Superintendent of Police Office 2) Self-declaration (Form - D - enclosed) 3) 7/12, 8-A along with the detail proposal.

*Application Process*:
1.The awards given by the Department of Agriculture are given for each year. Accordingly, the period from 1st January to 31st December will be considered as the Award Year Period.
 2.While submitting the award proposal, two colored passport size photographs of the applicant (without staples) on the back of which the name and contact number of the applicant should be given, enclosed in a small envelope and attached with the proposal.
 3.The application form in the prescribed format, self-declaration, applicants 7/12, 8-A, character certificate in original copy along with the necessary work report (in the prescribed format) should be attached with the proposal.
 4.While attaching the photo as evidence with the proposal, detailed information about the place of the event (village, taluka, district should be mentioned), date, subject should be written below the photo.
 5.For each award, all the details of the documents should be attached to the proposal in the same order as mentioned in the index and the page number should be mentioned on each attached document and that page number should be included in the index.
 6.In each case, a detailed work report on the work done by the applicant, innovative experiments, initiatives etc. must be attached to the proposal.
 7.The Agricultural Assistant should guide the applicant in preparing the proposal. Where the certificate of Agricultural Assistant is required, the Agricultural Assistant should visit the applicant's field before spiral binding the proposal and verify the matters mentioned in the work report. Accordingly, the certificate should be prepared as per Form-E and attached to the proposal. For this, the applicant should contact the concerned Agricultural Assistant.
 8.It is the responsibility of the applicant to submit a complete proposal, accordingly the applicant should submit three copies of the complete proposal to the Taluka Agricultural Officer's Office.

state-farmer-international-tour:
*Id*:
75

*Scheme Code*:
state-farmer-international-tour

*Sponsor*:
State

*Scheme Provider*:
DSAO

*Scheme Name*:
Farmer's International study tours

*Main Component*:
Farmer's international study tour

*Introduction*:
The Agriculture Department is implementing the Farmer's International Study Tour scheme to make the farmers of the state aware of the developed and modern technologies of various advanced countries, as well as to make them aware of the export of agricultural products/marketing of agricultural products and market demand, agricultural product processing, and the latest technologies used in those countries and to help the farmers of the state to use them on their farms/farming, to provide information on agricultural technologies and related matters developed by various countries through direct discussions with the farmers of those countries, as well as through field visits, visits to relevant institutions, etc. To enhance the knowledge and capacity of the farmers, the scheme is being implemented by the Agriculture

*Benefits*:
B. Outcomes/Benefits of the tour -
1. Adoption of modern technology-
Farmers will be able to see and absorb new technologies firsthand from foreign farmers, agricultural institutions, research centers and industry,
Obtaining information about modern irrigation methods, greenhouse farming, organic farming, use of machinery, precision farming, use of artificial intelligence in the agricultural sector, etc.
2. Increase in agricultural productivity and quality -
To increase production and save costs by using methods that farmers have seen abroad and can adopt in their own agriculture. Obtaining information about producing quality products required for export.
3. Agricultural produce processing and value addition
To make farmers aware of the importance and methods of processing fruits, vegetables, grains, and dairy products.
Along these lines, to encourage the establishment of small scale industries at the local level.
4. Awareness of export and market demand-
Farmers are encouraged to engage in export-oriented agriculture by getting information about global market standards and certifications. It will be possible to obtain quality products based on information about all the necessary aspects of the export-oriented quality production process.
5. Increasing farmer's attitude and confidence
Direct discussions and exchange of experiences with farmers from different countries create a mindset to embrace new concepts.

*Eligibility*:
1. The beneficiary going for the study tour should be a farmer himself.
2. You must have 7/12 and 8-A copies of the current period (last six months) in your name.
3. The farmer should state in his self-declaration that his main source of income should be agriculture. (Form-2)
4. The farmer must have a Farmer ID under Agristack.
5. Only one person from a farmer family can avail the benefit of this scheme. Even if the selected beneficiary farmer wants to take other family members along at his own expense, it will not be allowed due to possible difficulties in travel management. A copy of the ration card should be attached along with it. (Family includes husband, wife and their children/daughters below 18 years of age)
6. The farmer must attach a copy of his Aadhaar certificate.
7. The farmer should be at least 25 years of age on the day of departure for the trip, there is no maximum age limit but it is mandatory for the farmer to be physically fit. For this, while submitting the application to participate in the said scheme, a medical certificate (at least from an MBBS doctor) should be submitted regarding physical fitness,
8. Even after receiving the letter from the Agriculture Department stating that the farmer has been selected for the study tour, the farmer should submit a medical certificate (at least from an MBBS doctor) stating that he is physically fit for the foreign tour. It is mandatory to undergo a corona test and submit such a report to the Agriculture Commissionerate. According to this report, it will be mandatory for the doctor to certify that the farmer is not infected with any infectious disease and that the farmer is physically fit to travel abroad for a period of 7-10 consecutive days.
9. The farmer should be a valid passport holder. A photocopy of the page showing the expiry date/validity of the passport should be attached as proof. The validity of the passport should be at least three months more than the date of departure.
10. The farmer should not be employed in a government, semi-government, cooperative, private organization. Also, he should not be a doctor, lawyer, CA (Chartered Accountant), engineer, contractor, etc. in the medical field. He should mention this in the self-declaration form. (Form-2)
11. The farmer should not have previously travelled abroad with financial assistance from the government (through any department of the central/state government, through agricultural universities and local self-government bodies). He should mention this in the self-declaration form. (Form-2)
12. The final authority regarding farmer selection will be reserved with the State Level Committee.

*Required Documents*:
7/12, 8A, adhaar card, Passport with validity of minimum 3 months, medical fitness certificate, ration card copy and application form

*Application Process*:
offline method - farmer can apply directly at TAO office in prescribed application format

FARM GATE PACKHOUSE

*Introduction*:
Horticultural crops in the state suffer from significant post-hectarervest losses, usually amounting to 20-40 percent of the production. Packing of fruit and vegetable products maintains their quality and reduces losses during transportation and distribution to the market. Farm gate pack house is required for hectarendling and processing of fresh fruits, vegetables, etc. to be exported. In farm gate pack house, fruits are packed before transportation or distribution.

*Benefits*:
1. Cleaning, grading and packing of the produced horticultural, medicinal and aromatic products in the field itself, to the required weight/size and temporary storage.
2.To extend the shelf life and quality of perishectareble goods such as fruits, flowers, vegetables, medicinal and aromatic plants.
3. To enhectarence the quality of the product by performing primary processing on the raw material without chectarenging its original form.
4. Reduce the number of intermediaries and ensure thectaret the direct producer gets a fair price and makes goods available to consumers at reasonable prices.
Subsidy is 50% of project cost - Rs. 240000/-

*Eligibility*:
1. farmers to hectareve an horticultural crop.
2.The beneficiaries of this scheme are individual farmers, groups of farmers, Self-Help Groups - which will hectareve at least 25 members, Farmers' women's groups, Farmers' Producer Organizations, registered agricultural organizations, farmers' cooperatives, cooperative marketing associations can be involved.

Restructured Weather Base Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)

Restructured Weather Base Crop Insurance Scheme (RWBCIS)

*Sub Component*:
Risk cover  against expected crop loss due to adverse climatic conditions like Heavy rain,low rainfall, long dry spell, unseasonal rains , low temperature, high temperature, humidity, wind speed, Hailstorm is provided . Sum insured per hectare is decided as per scale of finance for that crop.

*Introduction*:
1. This  Scheme ( RWBCI) is being implemented since Mrug Bahar 2016 in 30 districts of Maharashtra.It is implemented in Mrug and Ambiya bahar .The scheme is applicable for notified crops in a notified area (revenue circle). All farmers having orchard of notified crops in the notified area of given productive age are eligible to participate.
2. The Scheme is optional for both loanee as well as non-loanee farmers.
3. The scheme is being implemented through selected  crop insurance companies  through tendering process.
4. The claims are decided on weather triggers fixed .  The weather data is taken from  Automatic weather station established under Mahavedh project for the claims calculation.
5. Insurance is provided for expected crop loss due to adverse climatic conditions like Heavy rain,low rainfall, long dry spell, unseasonal rains , low temperature, high temperature, humidity, wind speed, Hailstorm.
6. One farmer can enroll for all notified crops upto 4 ha. Including both Mrug and Ambiya bahar.

*Benefits*:
Provide insurance coverage and financial support to Horticulture farmers in the event of notified crop failure in notified area due to adverse climatic condition .

*Eligibility*:
1. All farmers  growing notified crops of productive age  in the notified area.
2. The scheme is  optional for Loanee & Non-loanee farmers.

Sum insured per hector: Sum insured per hectare is decided as per scale of finance for that crop

Insurance Companies: The scheme is being implemented through selected  crop insurance companies  through tendering process.

3. Bank account details

pmfmfpe-common-infra-govt

Central

Ministry of Food Processing Industries

*Scheme Name*:
Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises

*Main Component*:
Common Infrastructure Facility

*Sub Component*:
Common Infrastructure Facility (Govt. Agency)

*Introduction*:
Support for common infrastructure would be provided to FPOs, SHGs, cooperatives, any Government agency or private enterprises. Common infrastructure created under the scheme should also be available for other units and public to utilize on hiring basis for substantial part of the capacity. Eligibility of a project under this category would be decided based on benefit to farmers and industry at large, viability gap, absence of private investment, criticality to value chain, etc. Credit linked grant would be available @ 35%. Maximum limit of grant in such cases would be as prescribed.

*Benefits*:
Credit-linked capital subsidy @35% of the eligible project cost with a maximum ceiling of Rs. 300.00 lakh per unit

*Eligibility*:
o Organization such as; Farmer Producer Organization/ Farmer Producer Company, Self Help Group (SHGs) and its Federation, Co-operative Societies and Government Agency.
 o Established or proposed to establish food processing line alongwith common infrastructure/ value chain/ incubation centers would be eligible.
 o Substantial capacity of common infrastructure as well as processing line assisted under the scheme should be available for use by other units and public hiring basis.
 o Total eligible project cost should not be above Rs. 10.00 crores.
 o Eligible project cost comprises of cost of Plant & Machinery and Technical Civil Work.
 o Cost of Technical Civil Work should not be more than 30% of Eligible Project cost.
 o Beneficiary contribution should be minimum 10% of eligible project cost.
 o There will be no pre-condition of minimum turnover and experience of the applicant organization.

*Required Documents*:
A. Detailed Project Report

 B. Compulsory Documents
 1 Aadhar Card of directors
 2 Audited Balance sheet with annexures of last 2 years along ITR, if not audited, self-attested will suffix.
 3 Bank Statement of last 6 month
 4 Board Resolution for availing loan /borrowing power /borrowing authority
 5 Copy of bye-laws along with its amendments, if any (Hindi/ English translation of byelaws required)
 6 DPR upload
 7 GSTIN Registration Certificate
 8 GST returns, if applicable
 9 List of Directors
 10 Machinery quotation with GST/Construction Estimates
 11 Memorandum and Articles of Association
 12 Ownership proof (title deed) of the business premise if owned by Entity
 13 Ownership proof (title deed) of the business premise if owned by FPC
 14 PAN card of directors
 15 Photograph
 16 Rent/ Leased agreement if applicable
 17 Resolution of Board of Directors for availing financial assistance from MOFPI

 List of Optional Documents
 1 Any other
 2 Clearance from pollution control board
 3 Copies of Compliance Report
 4 Copies of Licenses
 5 Copies of Licenses / permission granted by local authority
 6 Copies of Recent Grant Statements
 7 Copies of Shareholder Register
 8 Copies of Stock Register
 9 Last AGM and Board meeting copy
 10 Net-worth Statement of Guarantors
 11 Reply to deficiency raised

*Application Process*:
Visit the PMFME portal Link: PMFME Registration : https://pmfme.mofpi.gov.in/pmfme/#/Register-New-User.

  Step 1: Visit the Official Portal
 - Go to the official PMFME website: [https://pmfme.mofpi.gov.in](https://pmfme.mofpi.gov.in).

  Step 2: Registration Process
 1. Click on Login and select New Registration.
 2. Choose your applicant type: Individual, Group, or Common Infrastructure.
 3. Fill in basic details:
  - Name, mobile number, email ID
  - Aadhaar number
  - State and district
 4. After filling basic details, login credentials are sent by email and mobile no.
 5. Verify your mobile number via OTP.
 6. Set your password.
 6. Submit the registration form and log in using your credentials.

  Step 3: Filling the Online Application
 1. After login, click on Apply for PMFME Scheme.
 2. Complete the application form with the following sections:
  - Personal Information: Name, gender, age, contact details
  - Business Details: Type of enterprise, location, nature of food processing activity
  - Financial Details: Estimated project cost, proposed loan amount, bank details
  - Infrastructure & Machinery: Existing setup and proposed upgrades
  - Training & Capacity Building Needs
 3. Upload required documents:
  - Aadhaar card
  - Passport-size photo
  - Business registration certificate (if applicable)
  - Bank account details
  - Project report
  - Quotations for machinery
 4. Review all entries carefully before submission.

  Step 5: Final Submission
 - Click Submit to complete the application.
 - Youll receive an Application ID for tracking.
 - Save or print the acknowledgment for future reference.

  Step 6: Follow-Up and Support
 - Track your application status via the portal.
 - For assistance, contact the helpline: +91-9254997101 / +91-9254997102.

 Forgot/reset Password:
  User needs to click on Forgot Password on MIS login page.
  A popup will appear to enter USER ID and an option to send OTP either on email or mobile. After selecting, an OTP will send to the respective Email / Mobile.
  Enter the OTP received, new password, confirm password and captcha then click on Update button to change the password.

www.pmfme.mofpi.gov.in

*Disclaimer Text*:
Only Processed food products having followed FSSAI license and labeling norms are eligible.

 Following list of food processing activities are not eligible for assistance under the scheme.
 i. Trading and selling of unprocessed Millets/Cereals/Spices etc.
 ii. Unprocessed or Loose Milk (Selling of Milk/Curd)
 iii. Trading and selling of fruits and vegetables
 iv. Trading and selling of unprocessed Minor Forest Product
 v. Bee Keeping/Loose selling of Honey
 vi. Loose selling, trading and repacking of oil
 vii. Trading and selling of groundnut, Arecanut (Exception: Any proposal for export variety would be reviewed on case to case basis. State Government to take prior approval from MoFPI for such cases.)
 viii. Poultry, Piggery, Goatry or any other rearing activity of animals
 ix. Trading and selling of fresh Fish/meat/chicken etc.,
 x. Repacking of manufactures products
 xi. Canteen, grocery, hotel, tiffin services, restaurants or any other food services enterprises.

*Introduction*:
1. Introduction
 Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) launched Prime Minister Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme from 2020-21 to 2024-25 for unorganized sector of food processing. The expenditure under the scheme would be shared in 60:40 ratio between Central and State Governments.

 2. Objectives
 a. Enhance the competitiveness of existing individual micro-enterprises in the unorganized segment of the food processing industry and promote formalization of the sector
 b. Support Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), Self Help Groups (SHGs) and Producers Cooperatives along their entire value chain.
 c. Increased access to credit by existing micro food processing entrepreneurs, FPOs, Self Help Groups and Co-operatives.
 d. Integration with organized supply chain by strengthening branding & marketing.
 e. Support for transition of existing enterprises into formal framework.
 f. Increased access to common services like common processing facility, laboratories, storage, packaging, marketing and incubation services.
 g. Strengthening of institutions, research and training in the food processing sector.
 h. Increased access for the enterprises to professional and technical support.

Credit-linked capital subsidy @35% of the eligible project cost with a maximum ceiling of Rs.10.0 lakh per unit

 o No prior experience is required.

 Enterprises under Ministry of Food Processing Industries, Government of India is
 an important scheme that offers for formalization and mainstreaming the
 unorganized home based or micro food processing units. The scheme is useful
 for expansion of the existing units in terms of capacity and technology through
 installation of new machineries and additional civil infrastructures and Branding
 & Marketing on the principle of ODOP (One District, One Product). Branding and
 Marketing Support for Micro-food Enterprises /FPO's/SHG's/ Cooperatives holds
 prime significance as it paves way for each of them to not only reap benefits of
 scale but also become success stories that serve inspiration to all.

*Benefits*:
i) Support for branding and marketing would be limited to 50% of the total expenditure.
 Ii) Maximum limit of grant in such cases would be as prescribed.
 Iii) No support would be provided for opening retail outlets under the scheme.

*Eligibility*:
o Applicant should be an FPO/SHG/cooperative/ regional - State levels SPV to bring large number of producers together, are eligible to set up Branding & Marketing unit.
 o Following the ODOP approach, marketing & branding support would only be provided for such product at the State or regional level.
 o The proposal should relate to ODOP; Minimum turnover of product to be eligible for assistance should be Rs 5 crore.
 o The final product should be the one to be sold to the consumer in retail pack.
 o Product and producers should be scalable to larger levels.
 o Management and entrepreneurship capability of promoting entity should be established in the proposal.

*Required Documents*:
A. Detailed Project Report

 B. Compulsory Documents
 1. In case of SPV, an MoU between the two entities
 2. Certificate of incorporation
 3. Memorandum of Association
 4. Article of Association
 5. 2 zero balance current accounts. 1for applicants share of funds and the other for PMFME's share of grant.
 6. Trademark confirmation - application or certificate
 7. GST certificate of entity
 8. PAN of entity
 9. 3 year audited balance sheets of the entity.
 10. FSSAI license of the entity

*Application Process*:
Contact Disrict Superitendant of Agriculture Office / Agriculture Department

*Scheme Code*:
pmfmfpe-seed-capital

*Sponsor*:
Central

*Scheme Provider*:
Ministry of Food Processing Industries

*Scheme Name*:
Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises

*Main Component*:
Seed Capital

*Sub Component*:
Seed Capital

*Introduction*:
In the context of implementation of PM FME scheme, the
 Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) and Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD)
 have agreed to work together to ensure that SHG members are facilitated to avail the benefits
 under the scheme. The designated State Nodal Agencies (SNAs) appointed by the State
 Governments for this scheme and State Rural Livelihood Missions (SRLMs) under DAY
 NRLM would work closely for providing scheme benefits to the target beneficiaries.

*Benefits*:
i) Seed capital @ Rs40,000/- per member of SHG for working capital and purchase of small tools would be provided under the scheme;
 ii) Priority would be given for SHGs involved in ODOP produce in giving seed capital;
 iii) All the members of an SHG may not be involved in the food processing. Therefore, seed capital would be provided at the federation level of SHGs;
 iv) This would be given as grant to the SHG federation by SNA/ SRLM. SHG federation would provide this amount as a loan to the members of SHGs to be repaid to the SHG.

*Eligibility*:
aPriority would be given for SHGs involved in ODOP produce in giving seed capital.
 aAll the members of an SHG may not be involved in the food processing. Therefore, seed capital would be provided at the federation level of SHGs.
 a This would be given as grant to the SHG federation by SNA/ SRLM. SHG federation would provide this amount as a loan to the members of SHGs to be repaid to the SHG.
 aIn Maharashtra State; Maharashtra State Rural Livelihood Mission (MSRLM) and State Urban Livelihood Mission (SULM) regulated Self Help Group and its members only can avail this benefit.

*Required Documents*:
A) Three Step Approval;
 1. Self Help Group (SHGs) Approval
 2. Village Organisation (VOs)/Area Level Federation (ALF) Approval
 3. Cluster Level Federation (CLFs) Approval

 B) Online Application

*Application Process*:
Contact to MSRLM (for Rural) and NULM/SULM (For Urban)

*Source Url*:
www.pmfme.mofpi.gov.in

*Disclaimer Text*:
Only Processed food products having followed FSSAI license and labeling norms are eligible.

 Following list of food processing activities are not eligible for assistance under the scheme.
 i. Trading and selling of unprocessed Millets/Cereals/Spices etc.
 ii. Unprocessed or Loose Milk (Selling of Milk/Curd)
 iii. Trading and selling of fruits and vegetables
 iv. Trading and selling of unprocessed Minor Forest Product
 v. Bee Keeping/Loose selling of Honey
 vi. Loose selling, trading and repacking of oil
 vii. Trading and selling of groundnut, Arecanut (Exception: Any proposal for export variety would be reviewed on case to case basis. State Government to take prior approval from MoFPI for such cases.)
 viii. Poultry, Piggery, Goatry or any other rearing activity of animals
 ix. Trading and selling of fresh Fish/meat/chicken etc.,
 x. Repacking of manufactures products
 xi. Canteen, grocery, hotel, tiffin services, restaurants or any other food services enterprises.
pmfmfpe-training:
*Id*:
88

*Scheme Code*:
pmfmfpe-training

*Sponsor*:
Central

*Scheme Provider*:
Ministry of Food Processing Industries

*Scheme Name*:
Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises

*Main Component*:
Capacity Building (Training)

*Sub Component*:
Capacity Building (Training)

*Introduction*:
1. Training support would be provided to individual units and groups that are being
 provided support for capital investment. In addition, training support would also be
 provided to other existing units and groups in the districts that are processing
 ODOP products. Groups that are being provided support for marketing & branding
 under the Scheme would also be provided training support.
 2. The following are the focus areas for capacity building under the Scheme:
 i) Entrepreneurship development, essential functions of enterprise operations, marketing, book keeping, registration, FSSAI standards, Udyog Aadhar, GST Registration, general hygiene, etc.;
 ii) Specific training designed for ODOP product or the product produced by the unit including operations of necessary machines, hygiene issues, packaging, storage, procurement, new products development etc.;

*Benefits*:
i) Scheme provides Training along with Rs. 1250/- per District Level Committee (DLC) recommended beneficiary for completion of 3 Days training program on Domain Base and Entrepreneurship Development Program (EDP) @ free of Cost.
 ii) Scheme provides Training along with Rs. 460/- SNA approved Beneficiary who received Seed Capital as a payout.

*Eligibility*:
Following Beneficiary are eligible for training;
 i) District Level Committee (DLC) recommendation, OR
 ii) SNA approved Beneficiary who received Seed Capital.

*Required Documents*:
Documents are not necessory.
 Applicant must received recommendation from District level Committee (DLC) who apply for credit subsidy and SNA approval for Seed Capital.

*Application Process*:
Contact Disrict Superitendant of Agriculture Office / Agriculture Department
